message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step evaluation plan:
1) Identify each main node and its scope.
   - REG-031: Art13(7)(vi) requires, when appropriate, specifications for input data or other relevant information regarding training/validation/testing datasets, considering the intended purpose.
   - REG-028: Art13(4)(ii) requires disclosure of the expected level of accuracy (including its metrics), robustness, and cybersecurity as referred to in Art15, as well as known/foreseeable circumstances impacting those levels, and that the system has been tested/validated against these.

2) Map cross-references:
   - REG-031: No explicit cross-references provided in the node text; none to include.
   - REG-028: Explicitly references Article 15. Provided referenced nodes REG-038 to REG-042 elaborate:
     • REG-038 (Art15(1)): appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity throughout lifecycle.
     • REG-039 (Art15(2)): development of benchmarks and methodologies for measuring.
     • REG-040 (Art15(3)): declare accuracy levels and metrics in instructions for use.
     • REG-041 (Art15(4)): robustness requirements, error/fault resilience, mitigation of feedback loops.
     • REG-042 (Art15(5)): cybersecurity resilience against specific AI vulnerabilities and relevant measures.

3) Compare the policy chunk against each combined requirement:
   - Policy chunk content: defines “Responsible Release Criteria” including (1) problem definition for intended use, (2) performance metrics and their criteria, (3) error types and their criteria; and requires an evaluation plan for each metric and error type.

   - For REG-031 (Art13(7)(vi)):
     • The policy does not mention input data specifications or details about training/validation/testing datasets. This is an omission rather than a contradiction. Under the rules, omissions must be treated as Non-Deviation. No narrowing or conflict is present.

   - For REG-028 (Art13(4)(ii) with Art15(1)-(5)):
     • The policy references performance metrics and evaluation plans, which is compatible with declaring/validating accuracy levels and metrics (Art15(3)). It does not address robustness/cybersecurity levels or foreseeable circumstances affecting them; those are omissions. It also does not contradict requirements for lifecycle performance, robustness measures, or cybersecurity measures. No change in timeframes, responsibilities, sequencing, or negations are stated. Therefore, no deviation can be established.

4) Evidence rule:
   - No deviations are claimed; thus, no GDPR/AI Act quotations needed inside deviation objects. If a deviation had been claimed, exact quotes from the provided nodes would have been required.

Conclusion:
   - REG-031: Non-Deviation due to omission only.
   - REG-028: Non-Deviation; policy is compatible with parts of the requirement (metrics/evaluation) and silent on others (robustness/cybersecurity/circumstances), which remains a Non-Deviation under the task rules.
</deliberation>

    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
